description: One of a series of foley recordings I made by sticking a piece of contact (sticky plastic of the kind you use to cover books) to a wall and tearing various household objects off it. Recorded with a Rode NT1 into a Universal Audio Arrow. This sound comes from a paper docket (called a receipt in US English).

description: A second attempt at recording the deep mud I keep finding myself walking through each morning on Walthamstow marshes. This recording was made using a Sound Devices MixPre6ii and a stereo pair of FEL EM172 mics (connected to a bent wire clothes hanger and positioned about 30cm above my feet). Processing: Normalize -1.0db, Low cut filter 100Hz, Minor noise reduction to reduce background traffic/train noise. I do not require any credit or attribution. If any of these sounds have been of help, and you are feeling charitable, please do consider donating to Freesound to help keep the site running (a link is also on the home page). Any donations are greatly appreciated!

original-tags: ambient, feet, foley, foot, footstep, london, mud, muddy, step, walk, walthamstow, water, wet
description: A second attempt at recording the deep mud I keep finding myself walking through each morning on Walthamstow marshes. This recording was made using a Sound Devices MixPre6ii and a stereo pair of FEL EM172 mics (connected to a bent wire clothes hanger and positioned about 30cm above my feet). Processing: Normalize -1.0db, Low cut filter 100Hz, Minor noise reduction to reduce background traffic/train noise. I do not require any credit or attribution. If any of these sounds have been of help, and you are feeling charitable, please do consider donating to Freesound to help keep the site running (a link is also on the home page). Any donations are greatly appreciated!

name: 00511.wav
original-tags: button, light, switch
description: Macro: The audio file captures a series of mechanical clicks associated with a light switch being turned on at varying speeds. Meso: Includes a deliberate and slow click, a standard-paced click, and a rapid succession of clicks. Micro: The slow click has a cautious tonal quality, the normal click is confident, and the fast clicks convey urgency. Technical: Recorded with a Rode NT1-A microphone and a Focusrite Scarlett 3rd Gen Solo audio interface to capture the mechanical sound with precision. No post-processing was applied to retain the natural switch sounds. Additional Info: From an indoor domestic setting, this sound is suitable for adding realistic switch sounds in audio dramas or movies. The recording captures the tactile feel of an actual light switch in use.

description: Paper Ripping (Normal, Slow & Fast) Macro: This audio file encapsulates the sound of paper being torn at various speeds. Meso: It includes a steady, moderate-paced rip, a slow and meticulous tear, and a rapid succession of tears. Micro: The slower tear has a deliberate, fibrous quality, while the faster rips have a more abrupt and sharp sound. Technical: Recorded with a Rode NT1-A microphone and a Focusrite Scarlett 3rd Gen Solo audio interface to capture the intricate sounds of paper tearing. Additional Info: The sound was recorded in a quiet studio to ensure the clarity of the paper fibers tearing, suitable for use in post-production for film, theater, or other audio projects.

name: 00924.wav
original-tags: crunch, footstep, mgreel, morphagene, snow, winter
description: Recorded by Peter Speer. Formatted for use in the Make Noise Morphagene. Footsteps over ice and snow. Recorded in stereo on a Zoom H6, December 17, 2018 in Asheville, NC. This Reel has been divided into 24, evenly spaced 4-second Splices. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

I used a USB condenser mic (Samson CO1U Pro) with Audacity. The section of floor I dropped the shells onto was surrounded by acoustic panels to dampen the echos. Using only one side for mono. Notes : Cat on my lap, head on the desk where I placed my mic with one side aiming at her nostrils. Processing : Trimmed, in audacity 3.4.2. Probably normalized. Maybe some denoise (I turned off my computer at some point so didn't need denoise then) Tips : Recording is close and maybe sensitive to proximity effect. I suggest EQ-in the lows out a little and adding a tad of reverb for your project. Pros : this is adjustable to your needs :) Attribution is greatly appreciated but not required. Recommendation : X sound by Sadiquecat on Freesound.org in CC0 licence. a Hope you enjoy :) PS: Id love to hear how you use this sound, feel free to link your creations or share how/why you listen to this :)

name: 01263.wav
original-tags: comic, creak, creaking, creaky, game, moving, slapstick-sounds, squeak, squeaking
description: Okay, about this small bibliothek there is a story to tell. Maybe a year ago my mother phoned me and asked if I could give her a hand because the door to her kitchen was squeaking. After work I went to her, went down to the cellar, took the can with the lubricating grease, went upstairs and made my mother happy. Then I putted the grease back and went upstairs. Whe sat down, drank a cup of coffee. Then I had to go to the toilet and noticed that the toilet door was squeaking too. So I went down to the cellar again and took once again the grease. Now I thought, before I make the stairs again, I'll show if any other thing in my mothers house is squeaking. It was terrible! I swear, never in my entire life I've been in a house where so many different things were squeaking so impassionated. First off all I went back to my car and took my cheap dictaphone I use for work. And before I putted grease on those squeaking things I recorded them. xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx So this are really historic sounds! None of them exist anymore in the real world! xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx But ... when I heard through them later I was disappointed of the bad sound quality, cutting out higher ond lower frenquencies of the rich spectrum of those "Neeeeiiiks" and "Uiihggs". During winter holidays I processed them first with the audacity noise reduction - then with other tools. The result gives an unreal impression of the original sounding, they have now some "synthetic" touch - but makes them probably usable for funny films or comic-like effects. All the sounds in my "big squeek pack" where recorded in one afternoon and all in my mothers house. The "tension-sounds" for example are nothing else than a junker (tin can? - sorry, my english) moved from one side of a wooden shelf to another, meanwhile my dictaphone was laying in the junker. I don't ask to credit me - but if you make use of one of these sounds and create SOMETHING FUNNY with them, please let me know ... so I can show it my mother too. Thank you.

description: Shell casings being dropped onto a tiled floor. I got some shell casings from a local shooting range and recorded the sound of various sized casings bouncing on a tiled floor. I used a USB condenser mic (Samson CO1U Pro) with Audacity. The section of floor I dropped the shells onto was surrounded by acoustic panels to dampen the echos. A couple times the casing bounced into the mic stand, but I've left them in because they're still useful. There was some background noise caused by my computer which I removed with Audacity.

description: This is a sound I created for a film known as hard pan zingers (check it out on YouTube http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NK8f1Q8ARl4&feature;=plcp ) we needed a lot of bodies falling to the ground so we ended up creating a couple different sounds for it. One of the sounds was created by me literately throwing myself on a hard concrete surface, the other 3 were created by dropping a punching bag on the same hard cold surface. Part of the filmmakers Archive by Dane S Casperson A rich collection of creative commons sound FX and music created by a filmmaker for filmmakers. www.thefilmmakersarchive.com Equipment used: AT875r Cardioid mic run into a zoom H4N ~Dane Vandewiele

I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

name: 01820.wav
original-tags: button, light, switch
description: Macro: The audio file captures a series of mechanical clicks associated with a light switch being turned on at varying speeds. Meso: Includes a deliberate and slow click, a standard-paced click, and a rapid succession of clicks. Micro: The slow click has a cautious tonal quality, the normal click is confident, and the fast clicks convey urgency. Technical: Recorded with a Rode NT1-A microphone and a Focusrite Scarlett 3rd Gen Solo audio interface to capture the mechanical sound with precision. No post-processing was applied to retain the natural switch sounds. Additional Info: From an indoor domestic setting, this sound is suitable for adding realistic switch sounds in audio dramas or movies. The recording captures the tactile feel of an actual light switch in use.

description: The recordings found in this foley pack are all original recordings made by myself and they are intended for free public use to help content creators with their commercial or non-commercial projects. They are not intended for redistribution and misappropriation with monetary gain in mind. Microphones used: - Rode M5 Stereo Pair Processing applied: - Minimal Low-freq. filtering - Denoising where necessary These recordings were made with the best of intentions and with the limited resources so do not expect professional studio recorded foley. Thank you and enjoy!

name: 02203.wav
original-tags: adolescent, adolescente, alergia, allergie, allergy, chica, cold, erkaeltung, estornudar, female, fille, gesundheit, girl, health, human, jugendlich, maedchen, menschlich, niesen, ragazza, resfriado, rhume, salud, sant, sneeze, starnuto, teenage, ternuement, weiblich
description: This sound clip contains the sneezing of a young girl, triggered by a mild spring allergy. The sneeze is strong and clear, recorded in a quiet room with almost no background noise. It was recorded with a SONY Galaxy A 54 smartphone during an audio message. The background noise is insignificant and therefore unedited. This sound clip contains two sneezes of a young girl. The first sneeze is an unedited original sound that captures the natural sound quality and authenticity of the moment. The second sneeze was processed with a native compressor (ReaComp) from Reaper version 7.14 to smooth out the dynamics and emphasise the sound in the mix. This processing was intended to give the sneeze a more even volume and presence.

name: 02426.wav
original-tags: button, light, switch
description: Macro: The audio file captures a series of mechanical clicks associated with a light switch being turned on at varying speeds. Meso: Includes a deliberate and slow click, a standard-paced click, and a rapid succession of clicks. Micro: The slow click has a cautious tonal quality, the normal click is confident, and the fast clicks convey urgency. Technical: Recorded with a Rode NT1-A microphone and a Focusrite Scarlett 3rd Gen Solo audio interface to capture the mechanical sound with precision. No post-processing was applied to retain the natural switch sounds. Additional Info: From an indoor domestic setting, this sound is suitable for adding realistic switch sounds in audio dramas or movies. The recording captures the tactile feel of an actual light switch in use.

Use reverberation effect (Room size 50 ; Reverberance 60; Damping 50 ; Tone Low 50 ; Tone Hight 50 ; Stereo Widht 50 ; all other at 0) Gap the 2nd track to let it started after the cries and let it continue before cries ending. HOW CAN I DESCRIBE IT? (French)---------------------- // Typologie (Cf. Pierre Schaeffer) : X (Continu complexe) + V ( continu Vari茅) // Morphologie (Cf. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. name: 02997.wav
original-tags: beat, beating, blood, cardiac, chest, dumpf-dumpf, heart, heartbeat, organs, pumping
description: The sound of my beating heart...XY microphone pressed to my heart during a bit of a sprint and some weightlifting. One change that was done was low-pass filtering (chebyshev) to isolate it to lower frequency responses then amplified, giving it a characteristic bass. I refer to the sound as "Dumpf-Dumpf" as a phonetic reminder. The horrors of...cardiovascular exercise! Intended as a tension sound effect. Note: Listen with headphones or basswoofers as small speakers are unable to represent the frequency response in which to hear the sample.

description: Recorded by Peter Speer. Formatted for use in the Make Noise Morphagene. Footsteps over ice and snow. Recorded in stereo on a Zoom H6, December 17, 2018 in Asheville, NC. This Reel has been divided into 24, evenly spaced 4-second Splices. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

name: 03226.wav
original-tags: carpet, clean, close, closing, door, foley, handle, home, interior, office, open, opening, repeat, reverb, room, shut, shutting, slam, space
description: Recording of a door being opened and closed, and then opened and closed again. Very slight footsteps (on carpet) can be heard. The microphone (Sennheiser MKH-50) was intentionally placed about 5 feet away from the door and aimed slightly off-axis so as to capture more of the natural room sound/reverb. I have another recording available of the exact same door and mic setup, but aimed on-axis for a more direct recording (it still has a slight bit of room sound - that was what I wanted for these two recordings). Recorded into a Tascam 208i audio interface and into Adobe Audition. Very minor processing (low-cut at 80Hz to remove low-freq rumble).

name: 03479.wav
original-tags: audacity, bottle, breaking, broken, floor, glass, hit, impact, pieces, smash
description: This sound is a combination of two recordings, plus a synthetic tone. I recorded one glass slipping on a table, but since I did not want to actually smash it, I also recorded the sound of glass jars being thrown to the waste container. On Audacity, I applied a noise-reduction filter to both samples. Then I added a beat track and selected the long drip sound instead of the standard metronome tick. I used the paulstretch and amplification effects. This addition aimed at increasing the impact and trying to differentiate from the characteristic glass container sound.

name: 03509.wav
original-tags: clean, close, closing, door, doorknob, dry, foley, handle, metal, office, open, opening, repeat, shut, space, wooden
description: Recording of a door being opened and closed, and then opened and closed again. Very slight footsteps (on carpet) can be heard. The microphone (Sennheiser MKH-50) was intentionally placed about 5 feet away from the door so as to capture some of the natural room sound/reverb (which is very subtle). I have another recording available of the exact same door and mic setup, but aimed slightly off-axis to capture just a bit more room sound. Recorded into a Tascam 208i audio interface and into Adobe Audition. Very minor processing (low-cut at 80Hz to remove low-freq rumble).

name: 03510.wav
original-tags: bounce, bouncing, bullet, casing, dropped, game, gun, magnum, rifle, shell
description: Shell casings being dropped onto a tiled floor. I got some shell casings from a local shooting range and recorded the sound of various sized casings bouncing on a tiled floor. I used a USB condenser mic (Samson CO1U Pro) with Audacity. The section of floor I dropped the shells onto was surrounded by acoustic panels to dampen the echos. A couple times the casing bounced into the mic stand, but I've left them in because they're still useful. There was some background noise caused by my computer which I removed with Audacity.

I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

description: A second attempt at recording the deep mud I keep finding myself walking through each morning on Walthamstow marshes. This recording was made using a Sound Devices MixPre6ii and a stereo pair of FEL EM172 mics (connected to a bent wire clothes hanger and positioned about 30cm above my feet). Processing: Normalize -1.0db, Low cut filter 100Hz, Minor noise reduction to reduce background traffic/train noise. I do not require any credit or attribution. If any of these sounds have been of help, and you are feeling charitable, please do consider donating to Freesound to help keep the site running (a link is also on the home page). Any donations are greatly appreciated!

original-tags: ambient, feet, foley, foot, footstep, london, mud, muddy, step, walk, walthamstow, water, wet
description: A second attempt at recording the deep mud I keep finding myself walking through each morning on Walthamstow marshes. This recording was made using a Sound Devices MixPre6ii and a stereo pair of FEL EM172 mics (connected to a bent wire clothes hanger and positioned about 30cm above my feet). Processing: Normalize -1.0db, Low cut filter 100Hz, Minor noise reduction to reduce background traffic/train noise. I do not require any credit or attribution. If any of these sounds have been of help, and you are feeling charitable, please do consider donating to Freesound to help keep the site running (a link is also on the home page). Any donations are greatly appreciated!

description: Shell casings being dropped onto a tiled floor. I got some shell casings from a local shooting range and recorded the sound of various sized casings bouncing on a tiled floor. I used a USB condenser mic (Samson CO1U Pro) with Audacity. The section of floor I dropped the shells onto was surrounded by acoustic panels to dampen the echos. original-tags: crunch, footstep, mgreel, morphagene, snow, winter
description: Recorded by Peter Speer. Formatted for use in the Make Noise Morphagene. Footsteps over ice and snow. Recorded in stereo on a Zoom H6, December 17, 2018 in Asheville, NC. This Reel has been divided into 24, evenly spaced 4-second Splices. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

This recording was made using a Sound Devices MixPre6ii and a stereo pair of FEL EM172 mics (connected to a bent wire clothes hanger and positioned about 30cm above my feet). If any of these sounds have been of help, and you are feeling charitable, please do consider donating to Freesound to help keep the site running (a link is also on the home page). Any donations are greatly appreciated!

name: 04707.wav
original-tags: bathroom, door, drain, impact, shower, zoom
description: A recording of a shower door, I used this sound through Izotope Iris 2 to produce a low, slow bassy kick sound, for a pretty ambient piece I did. I don't know why I became fascinated with the shower door. Bathroom time can be surprisingly pensive, so perhaps the sound of the door shunting shut became associated with that introspective space for me. Yes, I am aware that this the second bathroom sound I have shared. This may take on a certain significance given the fact that I have only shared a handful of recordings, but I encourage you not to read too deeply in to this fact.

description: Recording of a door being opened and closed, and then opened and closed again. Very slight footsteps (on carpet) can be heard. The microphone (Sennheiser MKH-50) was intentionally placed about 5 feet away from the door and aimed slightly off-axis so as to capture more of the natural room sound/reverb. I have another recording available of the exact same door and mic setup, but aimed on-axis for a more direct recording (it still has a slight bit of room sound - that was what I wanted for these two recordings). Recorded into a Tascam 208i audio interface and into Adobe Audition. Very minor processing (low-cut at 80Hz to remove low-freq rumble).

name: 05180.wav
original-tags: paper, tear
description: Paper Ripping (Normal, Slow & Fast) Macro: This audio file encapsulates the sound of paper being torn at various speeds. Meso: It includes a steady, moderate-paced rip, a slow and meticulous tear, and a rapid succession of tears. Micro: The slower tear has a deliberate, fibrous quality, while the faster rips have a more abrupt and sharp sound. Technical: Recorded with a Rode NT1-A microphone and a Focusrite Scarlett 3rd Gen Solo audio interface to capture the intricate sounds of paper tearing. Additional Info: The sound was recorded in a quiet studio to ensure the clarity of the paper fibers tearing, suitable for use in post-production for film, theater, or other audio projects.

description: Recorded by Peter Speer. Formatted for use in the Make Noise Morphagene. Footsteps over ice and snow. Recorded in stereo on a Zoom H6, December 17, 2018 in Asheville, NC. This Reel has been divided into 24, evenly spaced 4-second Splices. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

name: 05637.wav
original-tags: action, armory, chik-chak, cocking, gun, guns, reload, rifle, slide, sound-effect, weapon
description: A chambering sound effect with low frequency filer "Cherby" bandpass amplified with hard limitation and mixed with dry/wet dial/slider to modulate the signal and finally, a small dashing of low dampened reverb, giving the produced sound effect of what could be a firearm operator holding the chambering slide back or the lever to load the ammunition into the frame of the gun., , The base foley article is a common office stapler, you'd be suprised how effectively similar it replicates the mechanical elements of guns when pitched around 50-65% of its original rate down. As a tip, if you were to pitch it up (or down from your own recordings) by that percentage, you'd get likely get the foley itself (try pitching up or down with some films, game sounds and even music and you can sometimes figure out what they use!)

description: The sound of my beating heart...XY microphone pressed to my heart during a bit of a sprint and some weightlifting. One change that was done was low-pass filtering (chebyshev) to isolate it to lower frequency responses then amplified, giving it a characteristic bass. I refer to the sound as "Dumpf-Dumpf" as a phonetic reminder., , The horrors of...cardiovascular exercise! Intended as a tension sound effect., , Note: Listen with headphones or basswoofers as small speakers are unable to represent the frequency response in which to hear the sample.

original-tags: bounce, bouncing, bullet, casing, dropped, game, gun, magnum, rifle, shell
description: Shell casings being dropped onto a tiled floor. I got some shell casings from a local shooting range and recorded the sound of various sized casings bouncing on a tiled floor. I used a USB condenser mic (Samson CO1U Pro) with Audacity. The section of floor I dropped the shells onto was surrounded by acoustic panels to dampen the echos. A couple times the casing bounced into the mic stand, but I've left them in because they're still useful. There was some background noise caused by my computer which I removed with Audacity.

description: This is a sound I created for a film known as hard pan zingers (check it out on YouTube http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NK8f1Q8ARl4&feature;=plcp ) we needed a lot of bodies falling to the ground so we ended up creating a couple different sounds for it. This sound is part of the filmmakers archive by Dane S Casperson A rich collection of creative commons sound fx for filmmakers by a filmmaker www.thefilmmakersarchive.com Equipment used: AT875r Cardioid mic run into a zoom H4N ~Dane Vandewiele

name: 06889.wav
original-tags: crunch, footstep, mgreel, morphagene, snow, winter
description: Recorded by Peter Speer. Formatted for use in the Make Noise Morphagene. Footsteps over ice and snow. Recorded in stereo on a Zoom H6, December 17, 2018 in Asheville, NC. This Reel has been divided into 24, evenly spaced 4-second Splices. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

This Reel has been divided into 24, evenly spaced 4-second Splices. I've found some great textures through playing the Reel back at 1x speed, with Gene-Size, Morph, Slide and Organize modulated by sequencers, random CV and cycling LFOs. Time stretching the Reel also leads to some fantastic new environments. Enjoy!

name: 07170.wav
original-tags: comic, creaking, creaky, game, moving, screwing, slapstick-sounds, squeak, squeaking, turning
description: Okay, about this small bibliothek there is a story to tell. Maybe a year ago my mother phoned me and asked if I could give her a hand because the door to her kitchen was squeaking. After work I went to her, went down to the cellar, took the can with the lubricating grease, went upstairs and made my mother happy. Then I putted the grease back and went upstairs. Whe sat down, drank a cup of coffee. Then I had to go to the toilet and noticed that the toilet door was squeaking too. So I went down to the cellar again and took once again the grease. Now I thought, before I make the stairs again, I'll show if any other thing in my mothers house is squeaking. It was terrible! I swear, never in my entire life I've been in a house where so many different things were squeaking so impassionated. First off all I went back to my car and took my cheap dictaphone I use for work. And before I putted grease on those squeaking things I recorded them. xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx So this are really historic sounds! None of them exist anymore in the real world! xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx But ... when I heard through them later I was disappointed of the bad sound quality, cutting out higher ond lower frenquencies of the rich spectrum of those "Neeeeiiiks" and "Uiihggs". During winter holidays I processed them first with the audacity noise reduction - then with other tools. The result gives an unreal impression of the original sounding, they have now some "synthetic" touch - but makes them probably usable for funny films or comic-like effects. All the sounds in my "big squeek pack" where recorded in one afternoon and all in my mothers house. The "tension-sounds" for example are nothing else than a junker (tin can? - sorry, my english) moved from one side of a wooden shelf to another, meanwhile my dictaphone was laying in the junker. I don't ask to credit me - but if you make use of one of these sounds and create SOMETHING FUNNY with them, please let me know ... so I can show it my mother too. Thank you.

description: A second attempt at recording the deep mud I keep finding myself walking through each morning on Walthamstow marshes. This recording was made using a Sound Devices MixPre6ii and a stereo pair of FEL EM172 mics (connected to a bent wire clothes hanger and positioned about 30cm above my feet). Processing: Normalize -1.0db, Low cut filter 100Hz, Minor noise reduction to reduce background traffic/train noise. I do not require any credit or attribution. If any of these sounds have been of help, and you are feeling charitable, please do consider donating to Freesound to help keep the site running (a link is also on the home page). Any donations are greatly appreciated!

name: 08195.wav
original-tags: floor, footstep, ground, step, walk, walks
description: Footsteps... NOTE: These are no field recordings but HANDMADE walking sounds in different speeds and manners intended for game creation. Most of them you can loop. They are recorded as dry as possible so you should add the ambience you like. HOW TO MAKE THESE: 1. First empty two bottles of the famous spanish brandy Lepanto. 2. Keep the korks - they have a very special sound different from the korks of wine bottles. 3. Then, if you're still able to hit (maybe you shouldn't drink the brandy alone and at once), fill things in a flat bowl or a plate - f. e. pepper grains or salt. 4. Step the korks in the bowl and record it. You may also - as I did - step the korks on other things like a ventilator. 5. Compress the sounds hard but limit them to -4 db (as they sound not naturally if they are to loud).

description: Door Slammed ! One of my brothers came by spontaneously to say hello and he slammed the door behind him LOL It was non-aggressive of course, the door is heavy and has to be slammed in order for it to be closed properly ! Little did my bro know that I was recording and that I managed to catch the door slam ! The house was dark, empty and quiet - he wondered wtf was going on LOL It sounds a bit distant, it's because I was in the bedroom and not in the entrance. This sound is natural too, which is perfect.
